title: Document how (skopeo inspect) is, and isn’t, platform-specific
body:
```
$ skopeo inspect docker://registry.redhat.io/redhat/redhat-operator-index:v4.8 --config
FATA[0001] Error parsing manifest for image: Error choosing image instance: no image found in manifest list for architecture amd64, variant "", OS darwin
```

with:
```
$ skopeo --version
skopeo version 1.3.1-dev commit: 714ffe1b606bdcfd18b3cf746df4c3eaf5006f1b
$ sw_vers
ProductName:	macOS
ProductVersion:	11.3.1
BuildVersion:	20E241
```

versus:

with:
```
$ skopeo --version
skopeo version 1.2.3-dev
$ uname -a
Linux dev-centos8-c2-4-16-500gb-std-disk-1 4.18.0-193.14.2.el8_2.x86_64 #1 SMP Sun Jul 26 03:54:29 UTC 2020 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```
`--raw` does work on MacOS, but that's not the info needed in this use case.

comments.body:
Thanks for your report.

This is expected: on a multi-platform image, the config can in principle differ between platforms, and `skopeo inspect` as a general principle displays information about the image matching the current platform. That’s admittedly sometimes surprising and inconvenient, but it’s now a long-standing behavior we have to live with.

See the various `--override-` options, e.g. `--override-os linux`.

This works well for me, thank you @mtrmac !:
```console
skopeo inspect docker://registry.redhat.io/redhat/redhat-operator-index:v4.8 --config --override-os linux
```
I can't find it specifically documented anywhere. I mean I found `--override`, but not with a list of acceptable arch names and how it would help if I was trying to use skopeo not from one of them.
That‘s a good point, we should definitely document what `skopeo inspect` does with multi-platform images better.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
